sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: The upcoming clash at Stade Francis-Le Ble in Brest features Stade Brestois, currently 9th in the league with 47 points, against Lille, who are positioned 5th with 57 points. This matchup promises to be an intriguing battle as both teams vie for crucial points in their respective campaigns. The venue, known for its passionate home crowd, could play a significant role in influencing the outcome of this game. With referee Thomas Leonard officiating, who averages 3.8 yellow cards per game, we can expect a tightly contested encounter with potential disciplinary actions.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: Looking at the last 5 matchups between Stade Brestois and Lille, the away side holds a clear edge with 3 wins, while 2 games ended in draws, leaving Brest without a victory. Their most recent encounter on December 6, 2024, saw Lille triumph 3-1 at home, with Jonathan David earning the Player of the Match honors with an impressive Sofascore rating of 8.6. Head-to-head streaks show Lille undefeated in their last 5 meetings, while Brest have failed to keep a clean sheet in 4 of those games. Interestingly, 6 out of their last 8 head-to-heads have seen under 2.5 goals, suggesting a trend of low-scoring affairs.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: Lille enter this match on a strong note, boasting a streak of no losses in their last 4 general matches. Meanwhile, Stade Brestois have seen over 2.5 goals in 5 of their last 6 home games, indicating a tendency for high-scoring encounters at home. Both teams have shown a knack for scoring first in 5 of their last 6 matches, which could set the tone early in this game. Additionally, both teams have scored in 5 of Brest’s last 6 home matches and in 4 of Lille’s last 5 away games, hinting at a potentially open game. Brest also have a streak of more than 4.5 cards in 5 of their last 7 home matches, reflecting their physical approach.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Stade Brestois
sections.text: For Stade Brestois, Ludovic Ajorque stands out as a key attacking threat with 12 goals this season, overperforming his expected goals of 9.60 by 2.4029. Romain Del Castillo, a creative midfielder, has contributed 6 goals and 3 assists, showing his importance in the final third. Another notable player is Massadio Haïdara, a defender who ranks 5th in the league for fouls drawn per game at 2.2, often disrupting opposition attacks. However, Brest will miss key midfielder Mahdi Camara, who has 5 goals and 3 assists, which could impact their midfield dynamism.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Lille
sections.text: Lille’s attack is spearheaded by Jonathan David, who ranks 3rd in the league with 16 goals, slightly overperforming his expected goals of 15.31. David’s recent form is also notable, with 2 goals and 2 assists in his last 5 matches, earning an average Sofascore rating of 7.04. Defensively, Alexsandro Ribeiro, with a league rank of 4th for aerial duels won at 75.6%, is a standout, and has a historical edge against Brest with an average Sofascore rating of 7.57 across 3 starts. Aïssa Mandi also impresses, ranking 3rd in the league for ground duels won at 71.2%. Lille will, however, miss Edon Zhegrova, who ranks 2nd league-wide for successful dribbles per game at 1.9, a significant blow to their wing play.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights - Stade Brestois
sections.text: Stade Brestois have scored 50 goals this season, ranking 7th in the league, but their defense has conceded 53 goals, placing them 15th for least goals conceded. They excel in headers, ranking 3rd with 9 goals from set-pieces, and in ground duels won at 52.02%, ranking 2nd league-wide. Their ability to create big chances is decent with 82 created (9th in the league), but they’ve missed 51 of them, also ranking 9th. Brest’s goal distribution shows a strong late-game push, with 22.64% of their goals coming between the 76th and 90th minutes. However, their over/underperformance against expected goals stands at -6.3575, ranking 16th, indicating some inefficiency in front of goal.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights - Lille
sections.text: Lille’s attacking output is led by their clinical finishing in key moments, with 30.3% of their goals scored between the 31st and 45th minutes, showcasing their ability to capitalize before halftime. Their defensive solidity is bolstered by players like Alexsandro Ribeiro, who has been a standout in past encounters with Brest. Lille’s recent away form shows both teams scoring in 4 of their last 5 games, which could expose vulnerabilities at the back. Their streak of less than 10.5 corners in 9 of their last 10 away matches suggests a focus on compact, controlled play rather than relying on set-pieces.

sections.subtitle: Interesting Facts and Stats
sections.text: Stade Brestois rank 2nd in the league for accurate crosses with 155, which could test Lille’s aerial defense despite their strengths. Brest also rank 4th for woodwork hits with 14, indicating a touch of misfortune in front of goal. Lille’s Jonathan David has been a consistent performer against Brest, highlighted by his 8.6 Sofascore rating in their last meeting. Brest’s Brendan Chardonnet, currently missing, ranks 4th in the league for percentage of long balls in total passes at 9.1%, showing their reliance on direct play at times. Lastly, Brest’s disciplinary record shows 62 yellow cards this season, ranking 9th for least received, but their fouls committed stand at 412, ranking 6th for most in the league.
